review_questions: Does CP00-143 cover exactly the 10 planned Risk A units in order?
Can any CP00-143 row load fixture payloads, materialize manifests, materialize golden/failure payloads, persist stable IDs, or execute replay commands?
Are Hermes evidence and Claude prompt rows descriptor-only and unable to write runtime state or send prompts?
Does the base tenant fixture row avoid reading internal fixture payloads or real tenant data?
Does CP00-143 hand off cleanly from CP00-142 to CP00-144 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

parsed_review.adjudication_notes:
PASS — CP00-143 Risk A fixture terminal boundary pack is internally consistent and introduces no P0/P1/P2 defects. Reviewed staged diff only (contract, README, new fixture-terminal-boundary-catalog.js, index.js re-export, audit.test.js, validate-rp03-audit-architecture.mjs). Verified each required check:

(1) UNIT COVERAGE — Exactly 10 units: RP03.P05.M05.S14–S22 (9) + RP03.P05.M06.S01 (1). CP143_UNITS array order matches the declared range; createAuditComplianceCp143CoveredUnitIds() yields first=RP03.P05.M05.S14, last=RP03.P05.M06.S01, all unique. validateAuditComplianceCp143Coverage cross-checks plan included_units count and order; the prompt confirms npm run rp03:audit:validate and node --test passed, so the plan binding agrees that M05 terminates at S22 and these are CP00-143's units. unit_count=10 is consistent across PACK_BINDING, contract.current_pack, contract.fixture_terminal_boundary_pack, validator assertions, and tests.

(2) RISK A NO-WRITE BOUNDARIES — risk_class 'A' set in PACK_BINDING, contract.current_pack, fixture_terminal_boundary_pack, and validated. All catalog functions are pure (no I/O, no mutation), return Object.freeze'd descriptors. The 44-field no_write_attestation is byte-for-byte consistent across CP143_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (catalog), contract.fixture_terminal_boundary_no_write_attestation, freezeNoWriteResult overrides, and the validator's Object.entries check.

(3) MATERIALIZATION — materializes_fixture_manifest / _golden_case_payload / _failure_case_payload all false; createAuditComplianceCp143FixtureTerminalBoundaryManifest() builds only pack metadata (binding+contract+readiness), not fixture data. deliverable_counts {fixture:2,test:3,hermes_evidence:1,implementation:4} and evidence_mode_counts {fixture_permission_audit_terminal_boundary:9, synthetic_fixture_base_tenant_boundary:1} match tests and validator.

(4) REPLAY / (5) STABLE ID — executes_replay_command=false, persists_stable_id=false everywhere; S22 (replay) and S21 (stable id) are descriptor rows only.

(6) HIDDEN FIELD LEAKAGE — hidden_source_fields list contains only field NAME categories (policy declaration), never values; consistent between catalog and contract. Run results assert unauthorized_count_exposed/unauthorized_object_name_exposed/hidden_field_names_exposed=false. Run-case output does not spread the row, so it does not even carry the name list.

(7) H03/C03 — hermes_gate=H03, claude_gate=C03 in PACK_BINDING flow through contract, readiness (h03/c03_gate_bound=true), Hermes packet (H03.CP00-143...), and Claude packet (C03.CP00-143...). Claude packet model='claude-opus-4-8', effort='max', read_only=true, executes_review=false, sends_claude_prompt=false.

(8) CP00-144 HANDOFF — next_pack_id=CP00-144, next_subphase_id=RP03.P05.M06.S02 across PACK_BINDING, contract.current_pack, fixture_terminal_boundary_pack, handoff packet, and validator. upstream_pack_id=CP00-142 / cp142_handoff_inherited=true preserved.

(9) HRX / (10) LDIP — splits_hrx_product=false and implements_ldip=false in all attestations; Claude review question explicitly reaffirms neither is performed.

ADJACENT CHECKS: schema_version bumped v0.9→v0.10 and matched in validator; 11 public exports declared in contract match the actual catalog exports and validator allowlist; index.js adds the re-export with no name collisions (module-private helpers slugFor/freezeNoWriteResult/buildRows/countBy/CP143_* are unexported, so export * is clean); case_id/catalog_id are unique (all 9 M05 case_ids share the rp03_p05_m05 prefix but differ by behavior_kind), so runAuditComplianceCp143FixtureTerminalBoundaryCase find() is unambiguous; STATUS_BY_KIND covers all 10 behavior kinds so no expected_status is undefined; JSON block is well-formed with a distinct key (no duplicate of prior CP142 attestation) and git diff --check passed.

NON-BLOCKING OBSERVATIONS (below P2, consistent with the established CP135–CP142 template; recorded for the record, no action required): (a) CP143_UNITS[*].index is unused metadata and the M06 row uses index:1 vs S-number indices on M05 rows — harmless since index is never referenced. (b) readiness.hermes_claude_boundary_declared and readiness.base_tenant_fixture_boundary_declared are hardcoded true but not asserted by the test or validator — trivial coverage gap. (c) Hermes evidence_refs lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-143/manifest.json, which is not in this diff; test and validator load it via try/catch with plan fallback, matching the prior-pack flow where the manifest is produced during closeout. (d) createAuditComplianceCp143HermesEvidencePacket marks every command status='passed' as a descriptor template — acceptable because the packet attests writes_hermes_runtime=false and emits_real_receipt=false, so it is explicitly non-authoritative. (e) Test passes Hermes command 'npm run rp03:audit:validate' while the validator passes 'npm run rp03:audit-architecture:validate'; neither asserts the string, so no functional impact.

Conclusion: staged diff is a clean, correctly-formed Risk A no-write closeout pack with no regressions to CP135–CP142 validation; approve.

No P0/P1/P2 findings — the pack covers all 10 planned units in order, the 44-field no-write attestation is consistent across contract/catalog/validator, and H03/C03 binding plus the CP00-144 handoff are correct. The only items noted are sub-P2 observations matching the established template.
